title: DNA/RNA Radiolabeling Protocol
author: Liz O'Brien
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8dshs6e

steps.title: Prepare G25 columns (from GE, green box)
steps.instruction: Prepare G25 columns (from GE, green box): vortex thoroughly, twist cap ¼ turn, snap off bottom, spin for 00:01:00 at 3000 rpm to get rid of liquid.

steps.title: Add 25 µL H 2 O...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL H 2 O to each labeling reaction after heat inactivation is done.

steps.title: Since 50 μl H 2 O...
steps.instruction: Since 50 μl H 2 O were in bottom of tube and you add your 50 μl reaction, you should end with up to 100 μl of 100 nanomolar (nM) labeled DNA/RNA.

steps.title: Measure 1 µL of each reaction...
steps.instruction: Measure 1 µL of each reaction with the black rad counter on shelf to get cpm readings.

steps.title: Heat inactivate the PNK at 65 °C for 00:20:00
steps.instruction: Heat inactivate the PNK at 65 °C for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Apply entire reaction (now 50 μl...
steps.instruction: Apply entire reaction (now 50 μl total) to G25 column resin.

steps.title: Add 50 µL H 2 O...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL H 2 O to a labeled eppendorf tube, place G25 column in it.

steps.title: Set up labeling reaction
steps.instruction: Set up labeling reaction: %s %s Mix the DNA, buffer, enzyme, and H 2 O at the bench, and then add the DNA/enzyme mixture to ATP-filled tubes in a radioactive use area.
